title: Stock signal diversity: per-stock momentum & drawdown differentiation

observation.what_i_noticed: All 12 stocks in the screener showed 'Buy' with zero signal diversity. Composite scores ranged 0.229-0.394 (spread=0.165, std=0.051). The signal engine was dominated by the market-level composite score (~0.165), with stock-specific adjustments too small to push any stock below the Buy threshold (0.15). In a bull market, every adjustment was systematically positive: analyst targets show upside, low-beta stocks get 'safer' crash bonus, high-beta amplifies the positive base.
observation.root_cause: get_stock_signal() used market composite as ~90% of the signal with tiny additive adjustments (~0.01-0.05 each). No stock-specific technical signals (drawdown, momentum) existed to differentiate stocks that are individually weak from those that are individually strong.

what_i_did.approach: Added two new stock-specific signal components to get_stock_signal: (1) stock drawdown signal — graduated mapping from stock's distance from its 52-week high to a -1.0 to +0.1 signal, weighted at 0.25; (2) stock momentum signal — the stock's own 1m and 3m returns mapped to a signal, weighted at 0.20. Updated all three call sites (screener, single stock endpoint, stock signal endpoint) plus the lab data generator to compute and pass stock 1m/3m returns from price_history.
key_design_decisions: Drawdown signal: near peak (+0.1), -3-8% (0), -8-20% (graduated linear to -0.8), below -20% (capped -0.6 to -1.0)
Momentum signal: 1m scaled by /12 (+-12% = +-1), 3m scaled by /20 (+-20% = +-1), blended 40/60
Weights: stock_drawdown=0.25, stock_momentum=0.20 — large enough to shift signals across Buy/Hold/Sell thresholds
New parameters are backward-compatible — default None values mean existing callers work unchanged

simulated_bull_market.note: Struggling stock (deep drawdown + weak momentum) gets Sell even in bull market

simulated_neutral_market.note: Full signal spectrum in neutral conditions

analysis.what_worked: Adding stock-specific drawdown and momentum as independent signal components with meaningful weights (0.25 and 0.20) creates real differentiation. A stock down 30% from its peak with -25% 3m return now gets Sell regardless of market direction. This is the correct behavior — the screener should distinguish winners from losers.
honest_limitations: Haven't tested with live screener data yet (requires network calls to Yahoo Finance) — simulation uses realistic but synthetic profiles
The new signals are additive, so in an extremely bullish market (composite > 0.35), even moderate drawdowns may not push to Sell. This is arguably correct — a bull market should have a bullish bias.

The drawdown signal and stock-specific crash risk signal overlap conceptually (both penalize stocks far from peak). However they measure different things: crash risk is about future probability, drawdown is about current technical position.
analysis.self_critique: Could have also considered relative strength (stock vs market returns) as a standalone signal instead of just absolute momentum. However, the current approach (stock's own momentum) is simpler and more intuitive to users.

next_steps: Run live screener to validate signal diversity with real market data
Consider adding relative strength index (RSI) or other mean-reversion signals for stocks in extreme positions
The 89% feature drift problem remains unaddressed — that requires model retraining, not signal engineering
depth_rating: targeted fix — narrow scope, high impact on user-visible screener quality
